name: Pepita and Almond Squares
method: Line an 8-inch square baking dish with parchment paper and come up about 3 inches on opposite sides. This will act as a handle to remove the squares from the dish.

In a large mixing bowl, add the almonds, oats, pepitas, cranberries, coconut, and hempseed. Mix well. Stir in the peanut butter and try to get it evenly combined. You can use your fingers when most of it is worked in.

Add the brown rice syrup, maple syrup, and vanilla to a small saucepan. Bring to a boil and continue boiling until it reaches the hard ball stage, 260°F, on a candy thermometer. When this temperature is reached, quickly pour over the almond mixture and stir well. It will start to harden up quickly. Pour into the prepared dish and press down firmly into the dish and as evenly as possible. Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
Grab the “handles” of the parchment paper and lift out of the dish. Place on a cutting sheet and slice into sixteen squares.
